what_it_does: Defines the CHANNEL LAYER seam for the fabric: live-session messaging, grouping, membership, and the single integration point with the memory system via channel-scoped recall. It is the lead-lane companion to UNIFIED-SEAM.
observations: A channel is a named, managed group with a member-set, attached context, and a coordinator, reusing the existing channels/gatherings concept.
The channel registry supports create, list, add-member, remove-member, and archive operations; a session or supervised clone can belong to multiple channels.
Members are reached through a unified per-member transport: HTTP push to a live channel session or supervisor /inject to a clone, so one broadcast can fan across mixed member types.
Joining a channel is a launch flag, not a wrapper command.
A SessionStart hook writes a rich profile (handle, cwd, model, self-description) to the registry.
A channel row carries an attachments manifest of sessions, docs, and recall_scope, handed to a member as loadable context on add-member.
The 5th wire is cc_channel op=recall, which runs the memory system's recall spine scoped to the channel's attached sessions using D-1 multi-space addressing.
Ownership is split: the lead owns the channel layer and the embed/rerank servers; the recollection/fork owns the recall spine.
It issues a correction to UNIFIED-SEAM: the self-approval standard is now APPROVAL-STANDARD.md, not HELD-self-approval.md.
standouts: The 5th wire is the sole, explicit integration between the channel layer and the memory system.
Tim-direct corrections are applied as the authoritative source for channel model details.
The file draws a hard ownership boundary between the lead lane and the recollection fork.
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with trust, author, date, verified, and relates fields.
Numbered sections with ownership and a today-vs-to-build state split.
Heavy use of project-specific terms: 5th wire, lead lane, fork, seam, fabric.
References companion documents and direct corrections rather than deriving everything inline.
concerns: It contains a correction directed at UNIFIED-SEAM that could drift if not applied there.
The to-build items are aspirational and not yet proven in the file.
Much of the design authority rests on Tim-direct corrections that are not reproduced in full inside the file.
Ownership claims may overlap with other seams unless the boundaries are machine-checkable.
notes: This file is the lead-lane channel-layer seam spec, pairing with UNIFIED-SEAM and pinning the channel-scoped recall integration plus Tim's corrections.
questions: What is the exact schema and API surface of the cc_channel registry?
What does D-1 multi-space addressing specify for project/session/segment scope axes?
How is the SessionStart profile hook installed globally across all sessions?
What are the precise semantics of recall_scope filtering and how is it enforced?
What constitutes the high-bar recorded intent condition in APPROVAL-STANDARD.md?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches; it is a design seam document that also issues a cross-document correction to UNIFIED-SEAM.
apparent_themes: Live-session grouping and membership as a first-class fabric layer.
Reuse over fork: existing channels/gatherings, unified transport, shared recall stack.
Scoped recall linking messaging context to memory retrieval.
Explicit ownership boundaries between lead lane and recollection fork.
Authority grounded in recorded direct corrections and approval standards.
intention_signals: Establish the channel layer as the live-session half of the fabric, align it with Tim's corrections, and define exactly one clean integration point with the memory system so the two halves can be built and owned independently.
novelty: The 5th wire framing, the attachments manifest as loadable channel context on join, and the unified per-member transport fanning across both live channel sessions and supervised clones.
contribution: Contributes the channel-layer seam design, the channel registry and membership model, the unified per-member transport, the profile-on-start hook, the attachments manifest, and the channel-scoped recall integration point that a fused system would keep as the live-session half's contract.
summary_for_embedding: CHANNEL-LAYER-SEAM.md is a design specification for the live-session channel layer (registry, membership, unified transport, attachments, profile hook) and its single integration point with the memory system via channel-scoped recall, companion to UNIFIED-SEAM.
intention_for_embedding: It was written to fix the channel-layer design against Tim's corrections and to draw a clean ownership boundary and single wire between the channel layer and the memory recall spine.
